title: AGGREGATE UDFs with more than 2 parameters incorrectly processed
poc: CREATE TABLE grouped_ints (value INTEGER, groupnr INTEGER, dbvalue double);
SELECT groupnr, aggr3(value, value, value) FROM grouped_ints GROUP BY groupnr;
SELECT groupnr, aggr4(value, value, value, value) FROM grouped_ints GROUP BY groupnr;
SELECT groupnr, aggr2(value) FROM grouped_ints GROUP BY groupnr;
SELECT groupnr, aggr2(value, value, value) FROM grouped_ints GROUP BY groupnr;
key_bug_features: AGGREGATE UDFs with more than 2 parameters
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, value1 INTEGER, value2 INTEGER, value3 INTEGER, group_id INTEGER NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (value1, value2, value3, group_id) VALUES (10, 20, 30, 1), (15, 25, 35, 1), (5, 10, 15, 2), (20, 30, 40, 2);
SELECT group_id, aggr2(value1, value2) FROM test_data GROUP BY group_id;
SELECT group_id, aggr3(value1, value2, value3) FROM test_data GROUP BY group_id;
SELECT group_id, aggr4(value1, value2, value3, value1) FROM test_data GROUP BY group_id;
